pi_items.source: Odoo 17 CRM documentation and task setup code (setup_task.sh line 35-37, 63). crm.lead is the standard model for opportunities and leads in Odoo CRM.

Task description (task.json line 3) explicitly states: 'Save this filtered view as a Favorite with the exact name: High Value Deals'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 16) which cleans up filters with this name, and verifier.py (line 45) which uses this as target_name.

Official Odoo 17 CRM documentation (expected_revenue_report.html) confirms 'expected_revenue' is a standard Monetary field in crm.lead model used to track estimated opportunity value. Confirmed in setup_task.sh (lines 45-53) where opportunities are seeded with expected_revenue values.

Task description (task.json line 3) states: 'Expected Revenue >= 50,000' (using comma as thousands separator). Odoo domain filter syntax from official documentation uses [('field', '>=', value)] format. The numeric value 50000 represents the threshold in standard integer format.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a saved filter in Odoo 17 CRM for the crm.lead model (opportunities). The filter must apply a condition on the expected_revenue field with a threshold of 50000 (using >= operator), and be saved with the exact name 'High Value Deals' as a Favorite in the search bar. The crm.lead model is the standard model for CRM opportunities in Odoo 17, the expected_revenue field is a standard monetary field used for opportunity value tracking, and Odoo CRM supports saving filters as Favorites in the ir_filters table. Test data is synthetically generated during setup with example company names and varied revenue values.
